factors.summary: Cintas is facing potential litigation and other contingencies, but management believes the aggregate liability will not have a material adverse effect.

evidence.text: Cintas is subject to legal proceedings, insurance receipts, legal settlements and claims arising from the ordinary course of its business, including personal injury, customer contract, environmental and employment claims. In the opinion of management, the aggregate liability, if any, with respect to such ordinary course of business actions will not have a material adverse effect on the consolidated financial position or results of operation of Cintas.

factors.summary: Forward-looking statements indicate that management provides guidance but does not guarantee outcomes.

evidence.text: Forward-looking statements may be identified by words such as 'estimates,' 'anticipates,' 'predicts,' 'projects,' 'plans,' 'expects,' 'intends,' 'target,' 'forecast,' 'believes,' 'seeks,' 'could,' 'should,' 'may' and 'will' or the negative versions thereof and similar words, terms and expressions and by the context in which they are used. Such statements are based upon current expectations of Cintas and speak only as of the date made. You should not place undue reliance on any forward-looking statement. We cannot guarantee that any forward-looking statement will be realized.

factors.summary: Cintas is experiencing organic revenue growth, with an 8.3% increase in total revenue and 7.5% growth in the Uniform Rental and Facility Services segment.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 6.7% for the three months ended August 31, 2019 over the same period in the prior fiscal year, from $1,698.0 million to $1,811.1 million. Revenue increased organically by 8.3% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2019 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2018

factors.summary: Cintas is successfully raising prices, contributing to revenue growth alongside increased sales volume.

evidence.text: Revenue increased organically by 8.3% as a result of increased sales volume. Organic growth adjusts for the impact of acquisitions, foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations and workday differences.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2019 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2018

factors.summary: Gross margins are improving, indicating positive margin trajectory.

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 49.0% for the quarter ended August 31, 2019, which was an increase of 110 basis points compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 47.9% in the same period of the prior fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2019 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2018

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with a significant increase in net cash provided by operating activities.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $276.9 million for the three months ended August 31, 2019, an increase of $113.9 million compared to the three months ended August 31, 2018.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are stable, with investments focused on expansion efforts in the Uniform Rental and Facility Services segment.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures were $64.7 million and $64.5 million for the three months ended August 31, 2019 and 2018, respectively. Capital expenditures in fiscal 2020 primarily relate to expansion efforts in the Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment, representing $53.0 million of the current fiscal year amount.

factors.summary: Cintas has completed the integration of G&K Services and continues to evaluate strategic acquisitions to broaden its customer base.

evidence.text: Cintas completed the acquisition of G&K Services, Inc. (G&K). The operations acquired in the acquisition have been integrated into the Uniform Rental and Facility Services operating segment. Cintas evaluates strategic acquisitions as opportunities arise.

factors.summary: Cintas is increasing shareholder returns through share buybacks, indicating management confidence.

evidence.text: During the three months ended August 31, 2019, under this new program, we purchased 0.8 million shares of Cintas common stock at an average price of $230.66 for a total purchase price of $193.1 million.

factors.summary: Cintas is investing in its workforce through increased sales representative productivity, indicating focus on talent retention and development.

evidence.text: Generally, sales productivity improvements are due to increased tenure and improved training, which produce a higher number of products and services sold.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2019 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2018

factors.summary: The economic environment is favorable, with strong demand across various end markets contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 6.7% for the three months ended August 31, 2019 over the same period in the prior fiscal year, from $1,698.0 million to $1,811.1 million. Revenue increased organically by 8.3% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2019 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2018
